Name: Exeggcute
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Even though it appears to be eggs of some sort, it was discovered to be a life-form more like plant seeds.

Name: Exeggutor
Effects: This attack does 20 damage times the number of [C] Pokémon your opponent has in play.
Flavor-Text: It is called “The Walking Jungle.” If a head grows too big, it falls off and becomes an Exeggcute.

Name: Wurmple
Effects: Search your deck for Wurmple and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: It lives among the tall grass and in forests. It repels attacks by raising up the spikes on its rear.

Name: Silcoon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It drinks dew that collects on its silk and waits for evolution. Its hard cocoon repels attacks.

Name: Beautifly
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon-EX.
Effects: You may have your opponent switch his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It has an aggressive nature. It stabs prey with its long, narrow mouth to drain the prey’s fluids.

Name: Cascoon
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It never forgets any attack it endured while in the cocoon. After evolution, it seeks payback.

Name: Dustox
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It violently flutters its wings to scatter toxic dust when attacked. It becomes active after sunset.

Name: Dustox
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Ancient-Trait-Effect: If your opponent’s Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack of this Pokémon, take 1 more Prize card.
Flavor-Text: It violently flutters its wings to scatter toxic dust when attacked. It becomes active after sunset.

Name: Nincada
Effects: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It can sometimes live underground for more than 10 years. It absorbs nutrients from the roots of trees.

Name: Ninjask
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, you may discard a card from your hand. If you do, discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Ninjask and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: Because it moves so quickly, it sometimes becomes unseeable. It congregates around tree sap.

Name: Shedinja
Effects: Put 1 damage counter on each of your opponent’s Pokémon. Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
This attack does 50 damage times the number of damage counters on this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A most peculiar Pokémon that somehow appears in a Poké Ball when a Nincada evolves.

Name: Tropius
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The bunch of fruit around its neck ripens twice a year and is delicious. It’s a highly favored tropical snack.

Name: Victini
Effects: Draw a card.
Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: When it shares the infinite energy it creates, that being’s entire body will be overflowing with power.

Name: Fletchinder
Effects: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tool cards attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The hotter the flame sac on its belly, the faster it can fly, but it takes some time to get the fire going.

Name: Talonflame
Effects: Draw 3 cards. You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: When attacking prey, it can reach speeds of up to 310 mph. It finishes its prey off with a colossal kick.

Name: Articuno
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 [W] Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: A legendary bird Pokémon that is said to appear to doomed people who are lost in icy mountains.

Name: Articuno
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 more damage for each heads.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: If your opponent’s Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack of this Pokémon, take 1 more Prize card.
Flavor-Text: A legendary bird Pokémon that is said to appear to doomed people who are lost in icy mountains.

Name: Wingull
Flavor-Text: It makes its nest on steep sea cliffs. Riding updrafts, it soars to great heights.

Name: Pelipper
Effects: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It is a messenger of the skies, carrying small Pokémon and eggs to safety in its bill.

Name: Pikachu
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can’t attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It raises its tail to check its surroundings. The tail is sometimes struck by lightning in this pose.

Name: Voltorb
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
This Pokémon does 60 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Usually found in power plants. Easily mistaken for a Poké Ball, it has zapped many people.

Name: Electrode
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
You may move all Energy from this Pokémon to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: It stores an overflowing amount of electric energy inside its body. Even a small shock makes it explode.

Name: Zapdos
Effects: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: A legendary Pokémon that is said to live in thunderclouds. It freely controls lightning bolts.

Name: Electrike
Flavor-Text: It stores static electricity in its fur for discharging. It gives off sparks if a storm approaches.

Name: Manectric
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It discharges electricity from its mane. It creates a thundercloud overhead to drop lightning bolts.

Name: Thundurus-EX
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack 30 more damage. If tails, your opponent’s Active is now Paralyzed.
This Pokémon does 50 damage to itself.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Natu
Flavor-Text: It usually forages for food on the ground but may, on rare occasions, hop onto branches to peck at shoots.

Name: Natu
Effects: This attack does 10 damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: If your opponent’s Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack of this Pokémon, take 1 more Prize card.
Flavor-Text: It usually forages for food on the ground but may, on rare occasions, hop onto branches to peck at shoots.

Name: Xatu
Effects: Look at the top 5 cards of either player’s deck and put them back on top of that player’s deck in any order.
Your opponent reveals his or her hand. Discard a Trainer card you find there.
Flavor-Text: They say that it stays still and quiet because it is seeing both the past and future at the same time.

Name: Shuppet
Effects: Discard a Special Energy attached to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It loves vengeful emotions and hangs in rows under the eaves of houses where vengeful people live.

Name: Banette
Ability-Effect: Each Pokémon Tool card in play has no effect.
Flavor-Text: Strong feelings of hatred turned a puppet into a Pokémon. If it opens its mouth, its cursed energy escapes.

Name: Banette
Effects: Your opponent can’t play any Pokémon from his or her hand to evolve his or her Pokémon during his or her next turn.
Put 5 damage counters on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: You may play this card from your hand to evolve a Pokémon during your first turn or the turn you play that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Strong feelings of hatred turned a puppet into a Pokémon. If it opens its mouth, its cursed energy escapes.

Name: Deoxys
Effects: If you go first, you can use this attack on your first turn. Draw 2 cards.
During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Overdrive Smash attack does 60 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: DNA from a space virus mutated and became a Pokémon. It appears where auroras are seen.

Name: Gallade-EX
Effects: You may have your opponent switch his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
This attack does 20 more damage for each of your remaining Prize cards.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Gallade-EX
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Gligar
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It flies straight at its target’s face then clamps down on the startled victim to inject poison.

Name: Gliscor
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Its flight is soundless. It uses its lengthy tail to carry off its prey… Then its elongated fangs do the rest.

Name: Binacle
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Two Binacle live together on one rock. When they fight, one of them will move to a different rock.

Name: Hawlucha
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Although its body is small, its proficient fighting skills enable it to keep up with big bruisers like Machamp and Hariyama.

Name: Absol
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench, you may move 3 damage counters from 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon to another of his or her Pokémon.
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: It appears when it senses an impending natural disaster. As a result, it was mistaken as a doom bringer.

Name: Inkay
Effects: Choose a random card from your opponent’s hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into his or her deck.
Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It flashes the light-emitting spots on its body, which drains its opponent’s will to fight. It takes the opportunity to scuttle away and hide.

Name: Jirachi
Effects: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck and put 1 of them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Discard all Energy attached to this Pokémon. The Defending Pokémon is Knocked Out at the end of your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It is said to have the ability to grant any wish for just one week every thousand years.

Name: Togepi
Effects: Your opponent draws a card.
Flavor-Text: A proverb claims that happiness will come to anyone who can make a sleeping Togepi stand up.

Name: Togetic
Effects: Shuffle 3 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.
Flavor-Text: They say that it will appear before kindhearted, caring people and shower them with happiness.

Name: Togekiss
Effects: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.
This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It shares many blessings with people who respect one another’s rights and avoid needless strife.

Name: Togekiss
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may look at the top 8 cards of your deck. Choose any basic Energy cards you find there and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: You may play this card from your hand to evolve a Pokémon during your first turn or the turn you play that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It shares many blessings with people who respect one another’s rights and avoid needless strife.

Name: Carbink
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Born from temperatures and pressures deep underground, it fires beams from the stone in its head.

Name: Klefki
Effects: Reveal cards from the top of your deck until you reveal an Item card. Put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: These key collectors threaten any attackers by fiercely jingling their keys at them.

Name: Dratini
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is full of life energy. It continually sheds its skin and grows steadily larger.

Name: Dragonair
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 60 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: A mystical Pokémon that exudes a gentle aura. It is said to have the ability to change the weather.

Name: Dragonite
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may heal all damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It is said to make its home somewhere in the sea. It guides crews of shipwrecks to shore.

Name: Dragonite
Effects: Attach up to 2 basic Energy cards from your hand to this Pokémon.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: If your opponent’s Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack of this Pokémon, take 1 more Prize card.
Flavor-Text: It is said to make its home somewhere in the sea. It guides crews of shipwrecks to shore.

Name: Altaria
Effects: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw a number of cards equal to the number of Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s).
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: On sunny days, it flies freely through the sky and blends into the clouds. It sings in a beautiful soprano.

Name: Bagon
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Dreaming of one day flying, it practices by leaping off cliffs every day.

Name: Bagon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Dreaming of one day flying, it practices by leaping off cliffs every day.

Name: Shelgon
Ability-Effect: Any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Its armored body makes all attacks bounce off. The armor is too tough, however, making it heavy and somewhat sluggish.

Name: Salamence
Effects: Discard any Stadium card in play.
This attack does 20 damage times the number of cards in your opponent’s hand.
Discard 3 Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It’s uncontrollable if enraged. It flies around spouting flames and scorching fields and mountains.

Name: Latios-EX
Effects: If you go first, you can use this attack on your first turn.
Prevent all effects of your opponent’s attacks, except damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Latios-EX
Effects: Discard 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Rayquaza-EX
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon can’t use Dragon Strike during your next turn.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Rayquaza-EX
Effects: Discard 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: Any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s {G}, {R}, {W}, or {L} Pokémon is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

Name: Hydreigon-EX
Ability-Effect: If there is any Stadium card in play, the Retreat Cost of each of your [N] Pokémon in play is [C][C] less.
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Reshiram
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, you may attach a [R] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your [N] Pokémon.
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When Reshiram’s tail flares, the heat energy moves the atmosphere and changes the world’s weather.

Name: Zekrom
Effects: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Concealing itself in lightning clouds, it flies throughout the Unova region. It creates electricity in its tail.

Name: Spearow
Flavor-Text: Eats bugs in grassy areas. It has to flap its short wings at high speed to stay airborne.

Name: Fearow
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It has the stamina to fly all day on its broad wings. It fights by using its sharp beak.

Name: Meowth
Effects: Draw a card.
Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Adores round objects. It wanders the streets on a nightly basis to look for dropped loose change.

Name: Dunsparce
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: It digs into the ground with its tail and makes a mazelike nest. It can fly just a little.

Name: Skarmory
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Despite being clad entirely in iron-hard armor, it flies at speed of over 180 mph.

Name: Taillow
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It dislikes cold seasons. They migrate to other lands in search of warmth, flying over 180 miles a day.

Name: Swellow
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It dives at a steep angle as soon as it spots its prey. It catches its prey with sharp claws.

Name: Swellow
Ancient-Trait-Effect: If your opponent’s Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack of this Pokémon, take 1 more Prize card.
Flavor-Text: It dives at a steep angle as soon as it spots its prey. It catches its prey with sharp claws.

Name: Swablu
Effects: Heal 20 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It constantly grooms its cotton-like wings. It takes a shower to clean itself if it becomes dirty.

Name: Altaria
Ability-Effect: Each of your [C] Pokémon has no Weakness.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: You may play this card from your hand to evolve a Pokémon during your first turn or the turn you play that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: On sunny days, it flies freely through the sky and blends into the clouds. It sings in a beautiful soprano.

Name: Rayquaza-EX
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon-EX, this attack does 50 more damage.
Discard the top 3 cards of your deck.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Rayquaza-EX
Effects: This attack does 30 damage times the number of your Benched Pokémon.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: You may play this card from your hand to evolve a Pokémon during your first turn or the turn you play that Pokémon.

Name: Shaymin-EX
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench, you may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
Effects: Return this Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your hand.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Shaymin-EX
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench, you may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
Effects: Return this Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your hand.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Pidove
Effects: Look at the top card of your deck. Then, you may shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon live in cities. They are accustomed to people. Flocks often gather in parks and plazas.

Name: Tranquill
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: No matter where in the world it goes, it knows where its nest is, so it never gets separated from its Trainer.

Name: Unfezant
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Shuffle all cards attached to each player’s Pokémon into that player’s deck.
Flavor-Text: Males have plumage on their heads. They will never let themselves fell close to anyone other than their Trainers.

Name: Unfezant
Effects: During your next turn, each of this Pokémon’s attacks does 80 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: You may play this card from your hand to evolve a Pokémon during your first turn or the turn you play that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Males have plumage on their heads. They will never let themselves feel close to anyone other than their Trainers.

Name: Fletchling
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Despite the beauty of its lilting voice, it’s merciless to intruders that enter its territory.

Name: Gallade Spirit Link
Description: Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Gallade-EX.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Healing Scarf
Description: Whenever you attach an Energy card from your hand to the Pokémon this card is attached to, heal 20 damage from it.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Latios Spirit Link
Description: Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Latios-EX.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Mega Turbo
Description: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Mega Evolution Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Rayquaza Spirit Link
Description: Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Rayquaza-EX.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Revive
Description: Put a Basic Pokémon from your discard pile onto your Bench.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Sky Field
Description: Each player can have 8 Pokémon on his or her Bench.(When this card leaves play, each player discards Benched Pokémon until he or she has 5 Pokémon on the Bench. The owner of this card discards first.)
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Steven
Description: Search your deck for a Supporter card and a basic Energy card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Switch
Description: Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Trainers’ Mail
Description: Look at the top 4 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Trainer card you find there (except for Trainers’ Mail) and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Trainers’ Mail
Description: Look at the top 4 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Trainer card you find there (except for Trainers’ Mail) and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Ultra Ball
Description: Discard 2 cards from your hand. (If you can’t discard 2 cards, you can’t play this card.) Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Wally
Description: Search your deck for a card that evolves from 1 of your Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-EX) and put it onto that Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward. You can use this card during your first turn or on a Pokémon that was put into play this turn.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Wide Lens
Description: Damage from the attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to is affected by Weakness and Resistance for your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Winona
Description: Search your deck for up to 3 [C] Pokémon and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Double Dragon Energy
Description: This card can only be attached to [N] Pokémon. This card provides every type of Energy, but provides only 2 Energy at a time, only while this card is attached to a [N] Pokémon.(If this card is attached to anything other than a [N] Pokémon, discard this card.)

Name: Winona
Description: Search your deck for up to 3 [C] Pokémon and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Energy Switch
Description: Move a basic Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: VS Seeker
Description: Put a Supporter card from your discard pile into your hand.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).
